findings.summary: PRE-EXISTING, unchanged by this diff: a bundle placed one level below any directory with a package.json silently prefers that foreign manifest over its baked ENGINE_VERSION (observed live: prints 9.9.9) — the old code misreported identically; follow-up could prefer the injected define when present

test flake surface: the mkdtemp bundle dir's ../package.json is the tmpdir root, so a stray /tmp/package.json from another process would fail the test spuriously — low probability, hardening is nesting the outdir one level deeper

ruledOut: proof-cannot-fail: cli.ts hunk reverted in isolation (test kept), rebuilt — exactly the new test red with the claimed ENOENT crash; restored, 4/4 and full suite 295/295 green

ADR-0057 'never hardcoded' violation: the injection chain verified end-to-end — build-bundles.mjs reads the repo manifest and defines __VFKB_VERSION__; the bundle's ENGINE_VERSION IS the manifest version by another route; the only literal is version.ts's honest 0.0.0-dev sentinel (pre-existing)
npm-layout regression: node dist/cli.js --version live-run identical to manifest; the try-branch is byte-identical to the old happy path (canary-proven surface untouched)
quiet-success: the test's equality assertion also catches broken define-injection (would surface as 0.0.0-dev != 0.2.1), traced not assumed
scope/protocols: 2 files only; ENGINE_VERSION import pre-existing; no AI attribution; no secrets; test style matches siblings
